michaelryan.struct.extendablearrays

Interface ExtendableArray<T>

  • Type Parameters:
    T - the type to be stored in the array
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    ExtendableArrayImpl

    public interface ExtendableArray<T>
    A wrapper for an array that can be extended as needed.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void add(int index, T item)
      Inserts the specified item into the array at the given index.
      void add(T item)
      Adds the specified item to the end of the array.
      T get(int index)
      Returns the item at the specified index.
      boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if the number of items in this array is zero.
      int itemCount()
      Returns the number of items stored in the array.
      T remove(int index)
      Removes the item at the specified index.
      void replace(int index, T item)
      Replaces the item at the specified index with the given item.
      Object[] toArray()
      Returns an array representation of this object.

    • Method Detail

      • get

        T get(int index)
        Returns the item at the specified index.
        Parameters:
        index - the index at which the requested item is stored
        Returns:
        the item at the specified index

      • replace

        void replace(int index,
             T item)
        Replaces the item at the specified index with the given item.
        Parameters:
        index - the index at which
        item - the item to insert

      • add

        void add(T item)
        Adds the specified item to the end of the array.
        Parameters:
        item - the item to be added

      • add

        void add(int index,
         T item)
        Inserts the specified item into the array at the given index. All items to the right are shifted rightwards by one.
        Parameters:
        index - the position to insert the new item into
        item - the item to insert

      • remove

        T remove(int index)
        Removes the item at the specified index. For convenience, this item is also returned.
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the item to remove
        Returns:
        the item at the specified index.

      • toArray

        Object[] toArray()
        Returns an array representation of this object. The returned array is "safe" such that no references to it are maintained by this object. All items are in their proper position based on their index in the collection.
        Returns:
        an array representation of this object

      • itemCount

        int itemCount()
        Returns the number of items stored in the array.
        Returns:
        the number of items stored in the array

      • isEmpty

        boolean isEmpty()
        Returns true if the number of items in this array is zero.
        Returns:
        true if the number of items in this array is zero
